blockingIssues.description: Reviewer-stage failures are explicitly treated as non-fatal, so canary can pass even when reviewer invocation fails. This violates the requirement that canary exercise and validate reviewer invocation, and report component-specific failure when a component fails.

Canary claims to run "checkpoint_1 only" but the implementer command does not constrain checkpoints. It runs `slop_code run --problem file_backup` for the whole problem, which does not satisfy the requested smallest-scope canary behavior.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Canary metrics always record cost as 0.0 and only parse pass_rate from checkpoint_results.jsonl, so reported canary spend is not actual spend. Cost limiting is delegated to slop-code flags, but telemetry in two_agent_metrics.json is inaccurate.

codeReview.summary: Canary mode adds preflight checks and invokes implementer/reviewer/eval stages, but two core behavioral requirements are not met. Reviewer failures do not fail the canary, and the implementation does not actually restrict execution to checkpoint_1/smallest scope.

issues.description: Reviewer failure marked non-fatal; canary can succeed despite reviewer failure.
Implementer canary command is not checkpoint-scoped despite checkpoint_1-only requirement.
Cost telemetry in canary metrics is placeholder-only and does not reflect actual spend.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-worker skill's verification procedure conflicts with mission AGENTS validator scope. The skill directs full-repo pytest/ruff runs, while mission AGENTS requires scoped validators only. This mismatch can cause unnecessary failures and worker churn.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md step 6 recommends `uv run pytest -q` and `uv run ruff check .`; mission AGENTS.md "Validator Scope" requires scoped commands under research/tests/. Worker handoff shows workaround via ignored tests.

evidence: Executed git show --stat and git show for commit 01e8fdfc5c336a4940ce5d6706fcc576f2617961.
Inspected research/runner/two_agent_runner.py canary sections via Read/Grep and commit-line extraction.
Inspected tests/runner/test_two_agent_runner.py canary tests via Read/Grep.
Listed problems/file_backup checkpoints and confirmed checkpoint_1..checkpoint_4 exist.
Loaded transcript skeleton entry from worker-transcripts.jsonl for session cc22be6e-2f36-4757-baa3-bf6ecfbbd435.
Read .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md, mission AGENTS.md, .factory/services.yaml, and .factory/library/architecture.md for shared-state assessment.
summary: Review result: fail. Canary mode has useful preflight/error plumbing, but it does not fully satisfy requested behavior because reviewer failures are non-fatal and checkpoint_1-only scope is not actually enforced.
